what_it_does: Records a minimal metadata attachment entry that links attachment id att-4d2fccac to a board item target in the dragnet-development channel, with an added timestamp.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
The target is a board:// URI pointing to item-bed5b395.
The added timestamp is ISO 8601 with a UTC offset.
standouts: The target uses a board://item-bed5b395 URI scheme.
The file is purely a typed, timestamped link record with no payload.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
References external entities with a custom board:// URI scheme.
Records provenance via an ISO 8601 added timestamp.
concerns: The file carries no description of what is attached or why.
The board:// target cannot be resolved from the file alone.
No author or lifecycle state is recorded.
notes: A bare provenance stub that records a board_items attachment pointing to board item bed5b395 in the dragnet-development channel.
questions: What content or artifact does this attachment represent?
Who created this attachment and what triggered it?
Is the attachment still active or has it been superseded?
purpose_vs_actual: The file has no explicit stated purpose beyond its metadata fields, and it exactly contains that metadata; it functions as a typed, timestamped link record.
intention_signals: To create a durable, timestamped association between an attachment identifier and a specific board item so it can be retrieved or audited later.
novelty: Uses a board:// URI scheme to target a board item rather than a conventional file path or URL.
contribution: Provides a dated, typed reference edge from attachment att-4d2fccac to board item bed5b395 in the dragnet-development channel, preserving when the link was added.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata record att-4d2fccac of type board_items in the dragnet-development channel, linking to board item bed5b395 and recording an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To persist a timestamped association between an attachment identifier and a specific board item for channel memory.
